summary: Iteration 105: Release Events System - VERIFIED. All 27 backend tests passed. The dynamic release events system is fully implemented with 19 unique events (8 positive, 7 negative, 4 neutral) across 3 rarity levels (common, uncommon, rare). The generate_release_event function correctly generates events with personalized descriptions, quality/revenue modifiers, and quality-based probability bias. Frontend code review confirms proper display logic with colored borders, RARO badge, and nothing_special hiding.

success_rate.frontend: Code review passed - no runtime test (no films in shooting status)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Release Events System fully verified via backend tests. The system generates random narrative events when films are released. Events are stored in film_doc['release_event'] and returned in the release API response. Frontend displays events in the release result card with colored borders (green=positive, red=negative, amber=neutral). Events with id='nothing_special' are hidden in UI. Rare events show 'RARO' badge. Note: Full end-to-end release flow could not be tested as user has no films in 'shooting' status.

generate_release_event_function.quality_bias: Higher quality films have higher chance of positive events

frontend_display.nothing_special_hidden: Events with id='nothing_special' are not displayed

notable_events.nothing_special: Common neutral, 0 quality, 0% revenue (hidden in UI)

notes: All release events features are correctly implemented. The system generates dynamic narrative events (scandal, viral success, positive reviews, public flop, festival selection, etc.) that modify quality score and revenue when a film is released. Events are personalized with the film title. The frontend correctly displays events with colored borders based on type and shows the RARO badge for rare events. Events with id='nothing_special' are hidden in the UI as specified.
